text: Body mass index is a significant predictor of half marathon race time in recreational male runners, with higher BMI associated with slower times.
Speed during training sessions is a significant predictor of half marathon race time, with faster training speeds associated with faster race times.
Half marathon race time can be partially predicted using the equation: Race time (min) = 72.91 + 3.045 × BMI (kg/m²) - 3.884 × training speed (km/h), explaining 44% of variance.
Skinfold thickness at the suprailiac and medial calf sites is associated with half marathon race time, suggesting that body composition beyond BMI plays a role in performance.
Half marathon race time prediction requires both anthropometric and training variables and cannot be reduced to a single predictor variable.
Recreational male half marathon runners complete the distance in approximately 104 minutes at an average speed of 12.7 km/h.
